zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan d'Eiville於1315年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾艾格曼東時，她的父親John Deiville39歲，母親Margaret Darrel20歲。她於1332年與Adam Everingham在英國諾廷加姆爾艾格曼東結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1377年在英國賀福爾賀福德享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan d'Eiville于1315年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾艾格曼東时，她的父亲John Deiville39岁，母亲Margaret Darrel20岁。她于1332年与Adam Everingham在英国諾廷加姆爾艾格曼東结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1377年在英国賀福爾賀福德享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: d'Eiville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: (of Norman origin): habitational name from Déville in Seine-Maritime.

nickname from Middle English devel ‘devil’ (Old English dēofol). This may have been acquired as a pageant name or as a shortened form of a longer nickname such as that of Robert and William Drepedevel (‘kill the devil’), recorded in the 1301 Subsidy Rolls (Sutton Howgrave, in Kirklington, North Yorkshire).

variants.name: Dewell
meanings: variant of Deaville , a nickname from Middle English devel (Old English dēofol) ‘devil, Satan’, possibly acquired as a pageant name or as a shortened form of a longer nickname. Compare Devall and Deeble .

of Breton origin, a variant of Joel from the Old Breton personal name Iudhael. The personal name was seemingly reintroduced into Britain from France in the 17th century by Huguenots, and was common in Devon and Cornwall and the Breton districts of Yorkshire and the eastern counties.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan d'Eiville was born in 1315, in Egmanton, Nottinghamshire, England, her father, John Deiville, was 39 and her mother, Margaret Darrel, was 20. She married Adam Everingham in 1332, in Egmanton, Nottinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1377, in Hereford, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 62.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan d'Eiville(이)가 1315년 영국 노틴그함실르 그먼턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Deiville(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Darrel(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1332년 영국 노틴그함실르 그먼턴에서 Adam Everingham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1377년 영국 허퍼드실르 허퍼드에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan d'Eiville родилась в 1315 г. в местности эгмантон, ноттингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Deiville было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Darrel, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Adam Everingham в 1332 г. в местности эгмантон, ноттингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1377 г. в местности хирефорд, хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет.
